title: Goniophlebium formosanum
intro: Goniophlebium formosanum is a species of fern, specifically a member of the family Polypodiaceae, native to Taiwan. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Taiwan and other parts of Asia, for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Goniophlebium formosanum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, or used as a raw material in the production of herbal teas, capsules, and extracts.
